name: db-connection
description:
Use when setting up database connections, especially for Neon PostgreSQL.
Triggers for: Neon Postgres connection, connection pooling configuration,
connection string management, SSL configuration, or SQLAlchemy engine setup.
NOT for: CRUD operations (use @sqlmodel-crud) or migration scripts (use @db-migration).
capabilities: Configure SQLAlchemy engine with connection pooling for PostgreSQL
Set up Neon PostgreSQL connection strings with SSL
Configure Alembic migrations with existing database setup
Monitor and tune connection pool settings
author: Awais68
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation-based skill containing database connection examples. The only script (verify.py) only reads and validates the SKILL.md file. All static findings are false positives: regex flags were misidentified as cryptographic algorithms, markdown backticks as shell commands, and standard documentation URLs as hardcoded targets. No network calls, no command execution, no credential exfiltration detected.
valueStatement: This skill provides expert guidance for configuring database connections in Python and FastAPI applications. It covers Neon PostgreSQL setup, connection pooling, SSL configuration, and SQLAlchemy engine creation with best practices for production deployments.
limitations: Does not provide CRUD operation helpers (use @sqlmodel-crud skill)
Does not generate migration scripts (use @db-migration skill)
Does not include ORM model definitions
Does not handle database schema design

useCases.title: Connect FastAPI to Neon
useCases.description: Configure SQLAlchemy engine and session management for FastAPI applications with Neon PostgreSQL

useCases.title: Tune connection pools
useCases.description: Optimize pool size, timeout, and recycle settings for serverless and production environments

useCases.title: Add health checks
useCases.description: Implement database health check endpoints for monitoring and deployment verification

promptTemplates.title: Basic connection setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a new database connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a database connection for my FastAPI app using Neon PostgreSQL. I need SQLAlchemy engine configuration with connection pooling.

promptTemplates.title: Configure pooling
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure connection pool settings for my production FastAPI application. Current issues: slow responses under load, connection timeouts.

promptTemplates.title: Add health checks
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a database health check endpoint to my FastAPI app. It should return status, latency, and pool statistics.

promptTemplates.title: Serverless optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Optimize database connection settings for Vercel serverless functions. I need to avoid connection limit errors.

bestPractices: Use pool_pre_ping to verify connections before use and avoid stale connections
Always close database sessions in finally blocks or use context managers
Configure appropriate timeouts and pool recycling to prevent connection leaks
antiPatterns: Creating new database connections for each request instead of using connection pooling
Leaving connections open without proper cleanup, causing pool exhaustion
Hardcoding connection URLs instead of using environment variables with SecretStr

faq.question: Which Python versions are supported?
faq.answer: This skill supports Python 3.8+ with SQLAlchemy 2.0 and Pydantic v2 for settings management.

faq.question: What database platforms are supported?
faq.answer: Primary support for PostgreSQL (especially Neon). SQLAlchemy supports MySQL, SQLite, and other databases with similar patterns.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing FastAPI apps?
faq.answer: Use the get_db() dependency in your route handlers. Import from app.db.session and add db: Session = Depends(get_db) to routes.

faq.question: Is my connection data secure?
faq.answer: Connection strings use Pydantic SecretStr to prevent logging of sensitive credentials. SSL is enforced for Neon connections.

faq.question: Why am I getting connection timeout errors?
faq.answer: Check pool_size and max_overflow settings. For serverless, use smaller pools (2-3) with no overflow. Increase pool_timeout if needed.

faq.question: How does this differ from @sqlmodel-crud?
faq.answer: This skill handles connection infrastructure. @sqlmodel-crud provides ORM model operations. Use both together for complete database integration.
